title: Gerunds and Infinitives
description: Navigate one of English's trickiest choices: when to use verb + -ing versus verb + to, how certain verbs change meaning with each form, and why prepositions always take the gerund.
sources: CEFR B2 descriptors (Council of Europe, Common European Framework of Reference)
Raymond Murphy, English Grammar in Use (gerund and infinitive; verb + -ing or to)
